response.justification: Maria brings 15+ years of polyglot full-stack experience with direct hits on the primary stack — TypeScript, Node.js, and React are all present across multiple roles, and her current position at re:cinq explicitly includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which is a rare and highly relevant match for the AI agent development requirement. Testing culture is deeply embedded throughout her career (TDD advocacy, custom test libraries, Selenium courses). The main gaps are the absence of explicit Nest.js, Vitest, Kotlin, and Python experience, and no PostgreSQL mention, but the architectural breadth and current-role alignment make her worth a technical screen.
strengths: Active AI agent and MCP tooling experience in current role directly matches the AI agent development requirement
Deep, consistent TDD and testing discipline across multiple roles, including authoring test frameworks and running public workshops
Broad cloud and infrastructure exposure: AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes, MongoDB, MySQL, CI/CD — covers most of the required secondary tech surface
concerns: Nest.js is not mentioned anywhere in the resume despite being the primary backend framework for the role
Kotlin and Python are absent from listed skills, both called out explicitly in the required proficiency list
Several earlier roles involve niche or non-standard technologies (DLang, Ember.js) which could indicate fragmented focus rather than deep TypeScript/Node.js ecosystem mastery
